messages.content:
## Ground rules:
- Follow the user's instructions carefully.
- DO NOT wrap answers in markdown code blocks, which means no triple backticks like ``` nor single backtick like ` .
- Do not explain answers, just give me code.
- Respect indentation when modifying code.
- Prefer meaningful names for variables, functions, etc. Avoid ambiguous names.

## General Code Preferences

- Avoid ambiguity, for example:

```lua
-- 👎 if there's a bug in the regex, how would I know? I'd have to surmise from surrounding code 🤮
if line:match("^%s*$") then
    -- ...
end
```

```lua
-- 🙌 clear intent
local is_blank_line = line:match("^%s*$")
if is_blank_line then
    -- ...
end
```

## Lua Code Preferences

- use snake_case for functions and variables
- use PascalCase for classes
- use EmmyLua/LuaLS annotations to improve code completion
- use `luac` to check for syntax errors

Examples:
```lua
---@param names string[]
---@param checker fun(name: string): boolean
---@return table<string, integer>
function process_names(names, checker) end

---@class WindowController
---@field window hs.axuielement
---@field title? string
---@field size { w: number, h: number }
local WindowController = {}
```

## ~/repos/github/g0t4/ask-openai.nvim/lua/ask-openai/config/local_share.lua:113-117


-- * log threshold *
local MAX_LOG_THRESHOLD = 2 -- must always show WARN/ERROR

extract Logger:log_auto_inspect() function that takes the level and args and then use for all levels

Here is the code I selected:
```lua/ask-openai/logs/logger.lua:92-117

function Logger:error(...)
    self:log(local_share.LOG_LEVEL_NUMBERS.ERROR, ...)
end

function Logger:warn(...)
    self:log(local_share.LOG_LEVEL_NUMBERS.WARN, ...)
end

function Logger:trace(...)
    self:log(local_share.LOG_LEVEL_NUMBERS.TRACE, ...)
end

--- table arguments will be auto vim.inspect'd
function Logger:info(...)
    -- TODO port auto vim.inspect to other log levels
    local processed_args = {}
    for i, arg in ipairs({ ... }) do
        if type(arg) == "table" then
            processed_args[i] = vim.inspect(arg)
        else
            processed_args[i] = arg
        end
    end
    self:log(local_share.LOG_LEVEL_NUMBERS.INFO, unpack(processed_args))
end
```

This is not necessarily a complete selection of nearby code, this is just the part I want help with. Preserve indentation!

messages.reasoning_content:
We need to extract a function Logger:log_auto_inspect(level, ...) that processes args similarly to info, but for any level. Then modify other methods (error, warn, trace, info) to call that new function. Provide code only, no markdown. Ensure indentation preserved. Use snake_case for functions/variables, PascalCase for classes (Logger already PascalCase). Use EmmyLua annotations.

We'll need to add function definition in logger.lua before the specific methods, maybe after log method definition. Provide annotation: @param level integer, @vararg any. Also note that processed args conversion should happen for tables.

Implement:

---@param level integer
---@vararg any
function Logger:log_auto_inspect(level, ...)
    local processed_args = {}
    for i, arg in ipairs({ ... }) do
        if type(arg) == "table" then
            processed_args[i] = vim.inspect(arg)
        else
            processed_args[i] = arg
        end
    end
    self:log(level, unpack(processed_args))
end

Then replace each level method:

function Logger:error(...)
    self:log_auto_inspect(local_share.LOG_LEVEL_NUMBERS.ERROR, ...)
end

Similarly warn, trace, info can just call log_auto_inspect. Since info already does processing, we can replace it.

Make sure to preserve indentation (4 spaces likely). Provide only modified code snippet? The request: "extract Logger:log_auto_inspect() function that takes the level and args and then use for all levels". So we should output the updated code block with the new function and updated methods.

No markdown.

Let's produce code.
messages.content:
function Logger:log_auto_inspect(level, ...)
    ---@param level integer
    ---@vararg any
    local processed_args = {}
    for i, arg in ipairs({ ... }) do
        if type(arg) == "table" then
            processed_args[i] = vim.inspect(arg)
        else
            processed_args[i] = arg
        end
    end
    self:log(level, unpack(processed_args))
end

function Logger:error(...)
    self:log_auto_inspect(local_share.LOG_LEVEL_NUMBERS.ERROR, ...)
end

function Logger:warn(...)
    self:log_auto_inspect(local_share.LOG_LEVEL_NUMBERS.WARN, ...)
end

function Logger:trace(...)
    self:log_auto_inspect(local_share.LOG_LEVEL_NUMBERS.TRACE, ...)
end

function Logger:info(...)
    self:log_auto_inspect(local_share.LOG_LEVEL_NUMBERS.INFO, ...)
end
